about:
I'm currently design engineering at [Browserbase](https://www.browserbase.com/). I studied Design & CS at Carnegie Mellon [(portfolio)](https://maayan-albert.squarespace.com/), where I built an [Artboard Oriented IDE](https://twitter.com/supercgeek/status/1230163240815955968) featured at [CHI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conference_on_Human_Factors_in_Computing_Systems). I've worked at [Apple,](https://www.apple.com/iwork/) [Google,](https://www.google.com/travel/) and scaled [Avenue](https://avenue.app/) (acquired by Clay) as founding engineer.

I like hosting [events](https://x.com/i/trending/1992484272687771743) for exceptional people in SF, investing in startups (so original), and nerding out about design tools — [DM me](https://x.com/maayanalbert) if you're interested in any of the above :)
philosophy:
My mom used to call me a [kamikaze walker,](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1SaMNKAsV2Ov6tc7SybPnKeUO5NLn8UNg/view?usp=sharing) because I learned how to walk by standing up, going as fast as I could, falling, and getting back up again.

Someone I admire [once said](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kYfNvmF0Bqw) the key to life is bumping up against its edges. I believe in order to bump up against the edges you must fall, so you might as well get used to it, and better yet, learn to love it.
default: I build simple, useful, and slightly magical tools for humans. Inspirations include [the Macintosh](https://folklore.org/0-index.html) and classic hits like the [paperclip and zipper](https://www.penguinrandomhouse.com/books/130244/the-evolution-of-useful-things-by-henry-petroski/).
